events.title: Wildfires devastating richer areas but fewer hectares burned globally – study
events.description: ‘Megafires’ in California, Canada, South Korea and Europe in 2025, but changes to farming slowed spread in parts of Africa “Devastating” wildfires ripped across the wealthier parts of the world in 2025, a study has found, even as globally, the area ravaged by flames fell. Catastrophic blazes claimed lives, homes and jobs last year in California, Canada, Europe and South Korea. But the 335m hectares burned was the second-lowest since 2002, the review found, largely owing to the expansion of African farms that have fragmented landscapes and hampered the spread of large savannah fires. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Wildfires devastating richer areas but fewer hectares burned globally – study

events.title: Ebola outbreak in DR Congo collides with conflict and hunger, WHO warns

events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ebola outbreak in DR Congo collides with conflict and hunger, WHO warns

events.title: Four more men freed from flooded Laos cave in hazardous rescue mission
events.description: Two still missing as divers make their way deeper into cave through muddy water and sharp rocks to find them Four more miners who were trapped in a flooded cave in Laos for 10 days have been freed by divers, but two people are still missing as rescuers continue to crawl through narrow, deluged tunnels and sharp rocks to find them. The first of the party of seven men was rescued on Friday in a perilous rescue mission which has required teams to drain water from the cave and navigate collapse hazards. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Four more men freed from flooded Laos cave in hazardous rescue mission

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Mounting attacks and Russian threats prompt emergency Ukraine meeting
events.description: The UN Security Council is meeting in emergency session on Ukraine, with Secretary-General António Guterres telling ambassadors following what Kyiv describes as the most devastating attack on its capital to date, that “the time for peace is now.” In the early hours of 23-24 May, Russia launched a massive barrage of missiles and drones against multiple Ukrainian cities. Moscow has since threatened further sustained strikes. Sharp divisions among Council members are expected, with European nations demanding an immediate ceasefire while Russia insists its strikes target only military infrastructure.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Mounting attacks and Russian threats prompt emergency Ukraine meeting

events.title: Ethiopia: 'One of the most rapidly growing economies in Africa, but wealth distribution is terrible'
events.description: Annette Young is pleased to welcome Dr. Douglas Yates, Africa Specialist and Political Science Professor at the American Graduate School of International Relations and Diplomacy. Amid ongoing conflict, will Ethiopia's elections be "free and fair"? While acknowledging that electoral procedures may function in parts of the country, he argues that conflict, exclusion, inflation, and uneven development remain the defining realities shaping Ethiopia's political landscape. Dr. Yates also revisits the dramatic shift in perceptions of PM Abiy Ahmed since he received the Nobel Peace Prize in 2019. What was once celebrated as a moment of regional reconciliation has evolved into a far more complicated political reality shaped by internal rebellions and regional rivalries.

events.title: 'Most extreme right-wing fringes of Netanyahu govt call for far greater military action in Lebanon'
events.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Dr Filippo Dionigi, Senior Lecturer of International Relations specialized in Middle Eastern politics at the University of Bristol. As Israeli forces push deeper into southern Lebanon than at any point since the end of their occupation in 2000, the seizure of Beaufort Castle has powerful symbolic resonance within the history of the conflict. And while the military campaign is directed at Hezbollah, Israel seeks to exert pressure through widespread civilian suffering and forced displacement of more than a million Lebanese "as a weapon" of war.

The warning came as the US, UK and Australia announced a new Aukus project to develop new underwater drone technology to protect undersea cables. Continue reading...

events.title: Typhoon Jangmi threatens Japan as Europe swelters
events.description: Powerful winds and rain expected in parts of Japan and Australia, while temperatures in Spain could hit 40C A powerful tropical storm is forecast to track near Okinawa, Japan, on Monday before moving towards the south-east of the country. Typhoon Jangmi (also known as Typhoon No 6) has formed within the monsoonal gyre over the Philippine Sea. A monsoonal gyre is a large, slow-rotating weather system that spawns typhoons through smaller vortices formed within it. This flow can intensify storms. Such typhoons are typically characterised by broad areas of low pressure and extensive wind fields, often without a distinct eye. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Typhoon Jangmi threatens Japan as Europe swelters

events.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Financial Times reporter Abigail Hauslohner about the funding of President Trump's Board of Peace to oversee Gaza's reconstruction.

events.title: Man wanted in deputy ambush caught after wildlife camera sighting and major manhunt
events.description: Michael Puckett, accused of killing Virginia Deputy Logan Utt in an ambush during a welfare check, was captured in North Carolina after a multistate manhunt and $60,000 reward.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Man wanted in deputy ambush caught after wildlife camera sighting and major manhunt

events.title: Voting suspended in parts of Ethiopia over security concerns
events.description: Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's party is expected to dominate the election as conflicts overshadow the vote.

events.title: Protest outside Kenya’s planned US Ebola quarantine centre
events.description: Hundreds rally against plan to send US nationals exposed to the virus to Laikipia Air Base.

events.title: Confirmed Ebola cases in Congo reach 282 as survivors describe their recoveries
events.description: The outbreak remains focused in Congo's eastern Ituri province. Congo has reported over 1,000 suspected cases with the Bundibugyo virus, which has no approved treatment or vaccine.

events.title: 'To start somewhere else is like a rebirth': Franco-American band Fleur bleu·e
events.description: A group formed in Paris but now based in the US have told FRANCE 24 that the move has been like a rebirth for their music. Fleur bleu·e were created by a couple who lived above a bar in Paris, but swapped that life for one in a small town in the woods of Pennsylvania. They sing in both French and English as their aim is to address their music beyond France. Their new album is called "Question Marked Upon the World". Fleur bleu·e spoke to us in Perspective.

events.title: Hundreds protest plans to quarantine US Ebola patients in Kenya
events.description: Hundreds of young demonstrators marched outside a military base in Kenya over US plans for an Ebola quarantine zone in the country.

events.title: Banning children from social media is not the answer, UN warns – platforms must be made safe by design
events.description: Blocking children from social media is no substitute for making platforms safe in the first place, the UN human rights office warned Friday, as it issued a 10-point framework urging governments and tech companies to go further and faster to protect children online.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Banning children from social media is not the answer, UN warns – platforms must be made safe by design

events.title: Litterbugs now face on-the-spot fines in Tokyo's tourist hotspot
events.description: Dozens of officials will patrol world-famous Shibuya as Japan responds to the impacts of overtourism.

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: Death of Congolese man renews scrutiny of race relations in Ireland
events.description: Yves Sakila died after being restrained by security guards ‘in broad daylight’ Irish authorities have agreed to a second postmortem on the body of a Congolese man who died after being restrained by shop security guards on a Dublin street, prompting an outcry and comparisons to the death of George Floyd . A forensic pathologist from England is to conduct an independent postmortem this week on Yves Sakila, 35, an alleged shoplifter who was pursued and pinned to the ground in the city centre on 15 May. The police force, An Garda Síochána, is investigating. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Death of Congolese man renews scrutiny of race relations in Ireland

events.title: U.S. strikes Iranian military sites. And, Trump's $1.8 billion fund faces scrutiny
events.description: American aircraft fired on a number of Iranian sites over the weekend, including Qeeshm Island in the Strait of Hormuz. And, Trump's anti-weaponization fund faces scrutiny in Congress and the courts.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 U.S. strikes Iranian military sites. And, Trump's $1.8 billion fund faces scrutiny

events.title: India news: 'Cockroach' party founder plans protests
events.description: Abhijeet Dipke says he will return to India on June 6. Meanwhile, Rahul Gandhi has alleged 'fraud' in exam markings, and Virat Kohli has fired Bengaluru to the IPL title. DW has more.

events.title: Young German talents impress in World Cup tune-up vs Finland
events.description: Deniz Undav and Lennart Karl delivered strong performances in Germany's last game at home before the start of 2026 FIFA World Cup.

events.title: Champions League: What Qatar has gained from PSG's success on the pitch
events.description: After winning back-to-back Champions League titles, Paris Saint-Germain have achieved the goal Qatar’s sovereign wealth fund set itself in 2011 when it bought the club. After investing a huge amount of money, a quest for soft power, and a series of setbacks, PSG is today a global brand. And the Paris side, along with its Qatari backers, are looking ahead to new challenges.

events.title: Tactical timeout ban and new VAR powers - major World Cup rule changes announced
events.description: Major rule changes have been announced ahead of the 2026 World Cup - including expanded powers for VAR, 10-second substitutions and a crackdown on tactical timeouts.

events.title: Five recover from Ebola - as Brazil investigates two suspected cases
events.description: Five people have recovered from a rare type of Ebola virus, the head of the World Health Organisation said - as suspected cases were investigated in Brazil and Italy.

events.title: Under fire, Kharkiv is already building for a peaceful tomorrow
events.description: Every day in Kharkiv begins with uncertainty: air raid sirens interrupt sleep; missiles strike residential neighbourhoods, industrial sites, and roads. Anxious citizens rush into metro stations during bombardments and children study underground.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Under fire, Kharkiv is already building for a peaceful tomorrow

events.title: UN honours blue helmets and calls for renewed investment in peace
events.description: Peacekeepers are serving in a dangerous era of mounting global tensions and tight resources. UN chief António Guterres is now calling for greater political backing and reliable financial support for ‘blue helmets’ who put themselves in harm’s way.

events.title: UN80 Initiative: ‘Critical new phase’ for UN reform effort
events.description: UN chief António Guterres has outlined further progress in the wide-ranging UN80 Initiative, a major reform and restructuring project aimed at making the global body more effective, agile and better equipped to respond to global challenges.

events.title: College students accused of inciting boardwalk chaos walk free as prosecutors pull plug, city demands answers
events.description: Charges against four college students accused of organizing a Rehoboth Beach takeover event have been dropped by Delaware's Department of Justice.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 College students accused of inciting boardwalk chaos walk free as prosecutors pull plug, city demands answers

events.title: Dead whale towed ashore in Denmark ahead of autopsy
events.description: "Timmy" was found dead off the coast of Anholt despite multiple attempts to rescue it.

events.title: Nicaraguan indigenous leader dies after three years in prison
events.description: Rights groups condemn the death of Brooklyn Rivera, 73, following his long arbitrary detention by the governing regime.

events.title: Indian PM Modi meets Myanmar military gov’t leader in New Delhi
events.description: Myanmar opposition groups criticise India for hosting Min Aung Hlaing, but India says engagement is best way forward.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Indian PM Modi meets Myanmar military gov’t leader in New Delhi

events.title: Residents flee Beirut’s southern suburbs as Israel orders strikes
events.description: Videos show gridlocked traffic in the southern suburbs of Beirut as residents rushed to evacuate the Dahiyeh area.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Residents flee Beirut’s southern suburbs as Israel orders strikes

events.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
events.description: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

events.title: How aid cuts are hampering the front-line response to the Ebola crisis
events.description: Aid workers in Uganda are watching an Ebola crisis unfold in neighboring Democratic Republic of Congo. They're doing what they can to prepare for an uptick in cases, but foreign aid cuts aren't helping.

events.title: Hungary's PM Peter Magyar moves to oust president
events.description: Hungary's new Prime Minister, Peter Magyar, has moved to oust the president, threatening constitutional action. Head of state Tamas Sulyok has refused Magyar's demand that he resign.

events.title: Germany news: Migration to Germany falls sharply in 2025
events.description: Fewer migrants are making their way to Germany, with net migration dropping to nearly half what is was. Meanwhile, a court in Düsseldorf has sentenced a man to life in prison over an Islamist attack. DW has the latest.

events.title: Ethiopia votes as ruling party eyes landslide win
events.description: Ethiopian Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's party is running unopposed in several constituencies. Opposition parties have warned that the elections are even less open than previous votes.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Ethiopia votes as ruling party eyes landslide win

events.title: Ukraine: How a kamikaze drone partially operated by AI is attacking Russian convoys
events.description: Drones piloted by artificial intelligence are now being deployed on the Ukrainian front lines, and while there has been much talk about them, there is still much that remains unknown. The US-made Hornet Drone, which is partially guided by AI, is at the centre of a new Ukrainian strategy to target Russian logistics.

events.title: 'More than 45' killed after blast at building storing explosives in Myanmar
events.description: Scores of people have been killed following a blast at a building in northeastern Myanmar.

events.title: Rescuers dig for bodies after a massive mining explosives blast in Myanmar kills at least 38
events.description: More than a dozen rescue and charity groups used excavation machinery to recover bodies following a massive blast from stored mining explosives in northeastern Myanmar

events.title: Karmelo Anthony’s self-defense claim could define murder trial in high school track meet stabbing: analyst

events.title: Ex-Iowa school superintendent sentenced to prison as an illegal alien found with firearms
events.description: Iowa's largest school district ex-superintendent sentenced to prison for illegally possessing firearms while living in the U.S. as an illegal alien.

events.title: Hezbollah's 'game changing' night-hunting weapon punches through Israel's defenses: expert

events.title: English cops cuffed teen stabbing victim after attacker claimed racial assault

events.title: Fireworks reportedly trigger mass horse stampede through Rome streets, injuring several soldiers

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color

events.title: Alarm at Mexico bill allowing elections to be annulled for ‘foreign interference’
events.description: Opposition says constitutional amendment would give bill ruling party carte blanche to overturn will of voters Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Amid fierce criticism from opposition groups, Mexico ’s senate has passed ‌a constitutional amendment to include “foreign interference” as grounds to annul election results in the country. The bill, which was presented by the country’s president, Claudia Sheinbaum, defines foreign interference as “illicit financing, propaganda, the systematic ⁠dissemination of misinformation, digital manipulation, and ⁠the intervention of foreign governments ⁠or agencies”. Continue reading...

events.title: Chinese dissident says he was berated by ‘pro-regime’ interpreter for UK police
events.description: Hong Qi, who orchestrated protest against Communist government, claims interpreter on 101 call launched political tirade A Chinese dissident who orchestrated an anti-government protest in China after fleeing to the UK has claimed that a “pro-regime” interpreter used by a British police force berated him when he sought help. Hong Qi, who made headlines last year after using a mobile phone while in the UK to remotely project anti-regime slogans on to a building in his home city, Chongqing, contacted police after discovering that his bank accounts had been frozen. Continue reading...

events.title: Labor to highlight ‘risk’ of One Nation to cost of living in bid to dent Pauline Hanson’s popularity
events.description: Labor MP Mike Freelander says party must continue to help under-pressure workers, including considering further income tax cuts Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Labor will seek to highlight Pauline Hanson’s record of opposing cost-of-living relief for working people as it tries to prevent One Nation further eroding its voter base. As the government grapples with how to deal with Hanson’s surge, one Labor MP predicted One Nation’s support had peaked and the rightwing populist party wouldn’t be able to replicate its polling success at a federal election. Continue reading...

events.title: After Russian missile strike, owners of Ukrainian coffee shop pledge to rebuild

events.ticker_label: 🪖 After Russian missile strike, owners of Ukrainian coffee shop pledge to rebuild

events.title: French doctors sound alarm over drinking water pollution
events.description: Pesticides, microplastics and so-called forever chemicals: that’s the cocktail of substances millions of French people are exposed to simply from drinking tap water. According to France's National Association of Health professionals, representing private practice doctors, at least 30 percent of the population was exposed to contaminated water at least once in 2024. FRANCE 24's Environment Editor Valerie Dekimpe tells us more.

events.title: Ethiopia votes in elections expected to give Abiy's party a landslide win
events.description: Ethiopians voted on Monday in parliamentary and regional elections expected to hand Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed’s ruling Prosperity Party a landslide win. There was no election in the Tigray region due to “unfavourable conditions”, and voting in the Oromo and Amhara regions were interrupted due to security problems, according to election officials.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Ethiopia votes in elections expected to give Abiy's party a landslide win

events.title: Israel seizes Crusades-era fortress in Lebanon as it expands offensive

Sulyok, who was appointed under former prime minister Viktor Orban, has refused to resign before his term ends in 2029.

events.title: Property prices may drop but it’s decades of policy failure, not the budget, to blame
events.description: Instead of relying on tax-enhanced speculation, investors must now look at established properties based on actual profitability – leaving space for first home buyers Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast For decades, Australia’s property market has been defined by relentless price rises, reinforcing the old adage that real estate investment is “as safe as houses”. events.title: If Cuba collapses, Trump will be forced to deal with the consequences of his actions
events.description: It's only a couple of months or so since I was last in Cuba, and conditions then were pretty dire: power cuts, large queues for bread and fuel, state-run grocery stores virtually empty, mounting piles of rubbish on street corners, and few foreign visitors.

events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.description: It started as a school project to promote local businesses in a remote Bavarian region. Now, the "Chiemgauer" currency helps to cut emissions.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly

events.title: Trump admirer’s surprise first-round win is a blow to Colombia’s traditional conservatives
events.description: Success of far-right presidential candidate, Abelardo de la Espriella, suggests some voters are ‘fed up with politics’ The far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella and the leftwing senator Iván Cepeda have just under three weeks to compete for the roughly 3.6m votes that did not go to either of them in the first round of Colombia’s presidential election. That is no insignificant number, given that De la Espriella’s lead over Cepeda amounted to little more than 670,000 votes – 43.7% to 40.9%. Continue reading...

events.title: Colombia’s far-right presidential candidate De la Espriella wins first round of vote ahead of runoff
events.description: Lawyer and Trump admirer has risen rapidly in the polls and will face Iván Cepeda in election runoff in three weeks A far-right lawyer and Donald Trump admirer will go head-to-head against leftwing senator Iván Cepeda in the race to be Colombia’s next president after he won a surprise victory in the first round of voting. With 100% of ballots counted, the outsider Abelardo de la Espriella secured 43.7% of the vote – just over 10.3m votes – compared with 40.9% (about 9.6m votes) for Cepeda, a philosopher and human rights activist who has served as a senator since 2014 and is backed by the current leftwing president, Gustavo Petro. Continue reading...

events.title: Linda Reynolds repays nearly $9,000 after partner billed taxpayers to visit son in Melbourne
events.description: Former Liberal senator initially labelled ruling by parliamentary expenses authority ‘patently ridiculous’, saying visits were necessary Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Former Liberal senator Linda Reynolds has paid back nearly $8,800 in parliamentary expenses after her partner claimed family reunion allowances to visit his son in Melbourne while en route to meet her in Canberra. Reynolds accepted the ruling of the Independent Parliamentary Expenses Authority (IPEA) but initially disputed their ruling, saying her partner’s visits to his son – her stepson – were necessary after “high profile (and false) 2021 workplace allegations made against me”, seemingly a reference to her long-running court dispute with former staffer Brittany Higgins. Continue reading...

events.title: Australia’s 178 billionaires are $25.7bn richer than last year as 3.7 million live in poverty
events.description: Oxfam finds the 20 richest Australians now hold more wealth than the bottom 3 million households, analysis shows Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The wealth of Australia’s billionaires increased by $25.67bn in the past year, equivalent to almost $50,000 a minute, according to new Oxfam Australia analysis of the 2026 Australian Financial Review Rich List. The anti-poverty organisation said the total wealth of Australian billionaires in 2026 has reached more than $686bn, while Acoss figures show 3,706,000 people live in poverty, including 757,000 children under 15 years. Continue reading...

events.title: Violent clashes give way to peaceful PSG parade after Champions League win
events.description: A huge crowd of supporters gathered peacefully near the Eiffel Tower on Sunday to celebrate Paris Saint-Germain's victory, which was marred by violent clashes overnight that led police to detain hundreds of people.

events.title: Right-wing candidate pulls ahead in first round of Colombia's presidential vote
events.description: Tough-on-crime outsider Aberaldo de la Espriella took the lead in Colombia's presidential race on Sunday night, setting up a runoff with Iván Cepeda, an ally of outgoing President Gustavo Petro.

events.title: Is 'nation-hopping' ruining German youth football?
events.description: Many young German footballers are choosing other countries over Germany. DFB Director Andreas Rettig is aiming to change this and is calling for a compensation fee.

events.title: Coups and crises shake African democracy
events.description: When Burkina Faso's junta leader Ibrahim Traore called for democracy in Africa to be "forgotten," it was more than a provocation — it signaled a political shift across the continent.

events.title: Taiwan opposition leader heads to US
events.description: KMT leader Cheng Li-wun is traveling to the US for a two-week visit. She has indicated she is "very interested" in meeting President Donald Trump but could face a grilling over her stance on China relations.

events.title: How China keeps North Korea's economy alive
events.description: Pyongyang relies on China for 95% of its legitimate trade; hacking, arms deals and remittances also help it stay afloat.

events.title: France intercepts Russian-linked oil tanker in Atlantic
events.description: France has intercepted a sanctioned tanker in the Atlantic Ocean as it steps up efforts to target Russia's "shadow fleet." Naval officials said the ship was sailing under a false flag.

events.title: DNA had one rule. Bacteria didn't get the memo
events.description: Every cell in every organism on Earth copies DNA the same way. Except one bacterial protein — quietly doing something scientists had never seen before.

events.title: Nicaragua: Indigenous leader Brooklyn Rivera dies in detention
events.description: The Indigenous leader and former lawmaker had been detained by Nicaragua's authoritarian government in 2023 on undisclosed charges.

events.title: The Lemon Twigs blend 60s pop with modern anxiety
events.description: In this edition of our arts24 music show, Jennifer Ben Brahim chats with sibling duo The Lemon Twigs. The Long Island natives could easily be mistaken for a British Invasion band, thanks to their unmistakable harmonies and retro blend of pop, folk and psychedelic rock.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 The Lemon Twigs blend 60s pop with modern anxiety

events.title: Japanese newspaper lends printers to zine makers, as physical subscriptions fall
events.description: As physical subscriptions fall, the Kyoto Shinbun Newspaper is offering an alternative use for its machinery - by lending it to zine makers. Zines or self-published do-it-yourself magazines are enjoying growing popularity in Japan, with thousands flocking to one recent Tokyo zine fair.

events.title: Europe’s fight to stay in the AI race
events.description: This week’s special edition of Talking Europe asks whether the EU is ready for the disruption that comes with Artificial Intelligence. It is the most important technological revolution of the century, and is set to impact every aspect of our lives.

events.title: Pro-Trump candidate takes lead in Colombia's presidential race
events.description: Pro-Trump lawyer Aberaldo de la Espriella leads Colombia’s presidential race after the first round of elections over the weekend

events.title: Nvidia bets on AI personal computers with new chip powering Windows laptops
events.description: Nvidia has unveiled powerful new chips to bring advanced artificial intelligence to Windows laptops and desktops

events.title: Venezuela’s ruling party unity cracks as Delcy Rodríguez shifts Chávez-era policies
events.description: Venezuela's ruling party is facing internal divisions after 27 years of unity

events.title: Some young Spaniards embrace Catholicism and can’t wait for Pope Leo’s visit
events.description: Traveling to Spain in June and France in September, Pope Leo XIV will find traditionally Catholic but now staunchly secular countries with an abundance of historic churches but few people in the pews

events.title: Patients find help with therapy donkeys at psychiatric hospital near Paris
events.description: Therapy donkeys are helping patients with mental health conditions at a psychiatric hospital near Paris

events.title: Polls open in Ethiopia's election that is widely expected to be won by the ruling party
events.description: Polls opened Monday in Ethiopia in an election that is widely expected to be won by the ruling party

events.title: Voting begins in Ethiopia where Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's party is expected to win a majority of seats
events.description: Voting begins in Ethiopia where Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's party is expected to win a majority of seats

events.title: Colombia's presidential election, in photos
events.description: Colombians voted to select a successor to President Gustavo Petro in an election Sunday, as armed guards and police kept watch. Candidates Iván Cepeda and Abelardo de la Espriella advanced to the second round. ___ This gallery was curated by AP photo editors. ___ AP photography: https://apnews.com/photography AP News on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/apnews

events.title: Indigenous leader Brooklyn Rivera dies in Nicaragua after nearly 3 years of detention
events.description: Renowned Indigenous leader Brooklyn River, who spent years fighting for the rights of his community and was imprisoned by the government in September 2023, has died
events.ticker_label: 💥 Indigenous leader Brooklyn Rivera dies in Nicaragua after nearly 3 years of detention

events.title: Borrowed boots, borrowed joy: Football thrives in Gaza camps
events.description: Matches organised by a former professional player are providing a brief respite from the harsh reality of life for the thousands living in overcrowded tents, schools or damaged buildings in the shattered Occupied Palestinian Territory of Gaza.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Borrowed boots, borrowed joy: Football thrives in Gaza camps

events.title: ICE arrests illegal alien who allegedly faked asylum claim based on homosexuality, became Indiana jail officer
events.description: A suspected illegal immigrant allegedly used a fraudulent asylum claim to enter the U.S. and was later hired as a corrections officer in Indiana, DHS says.

events.title: Scott Peterson's longtime lawyer claims 'new' evidence could force courts to revisit his murder conviction
events.description: Scott Peterson's lawyer claims new evidence could force another look at his murder conviction, ahead of a summer documentary about the high-profile case.

events.title: NYPD commissioner 'proudly' leads Israel parade as grand marshal; Mamdani breaks tradition, avoids event
events.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani chose to skip NYC's annual Israel Day parade as Gov. Kathy Hochul, Chuck Schumer and other political leaders attended the event.

events.title: FBI hunts most wanted neighbor next door who allegedly faked cancer, scammed loved ones for luxury getaways
events.description: A Pennsylvania woman is wanted by the FBI after allegedly faking brain cancer to scam friends and family out of thousands used for Australian vacations.

events.title: Mojtaba Khamenei touts new anti-US alliance as Gulf backchannels seep into Tehran: analyst
events.description: Iran's supreme leader launched a sweeping counteroffensive against President Donald Trump, seeking to rally Middle Eastern nations into an anti-American alliance, an analyst says.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mojtaba Khamenei touts new anti-US alliance as Gulf backchannels seep into Tehran: analyst

events.title: Anti-cartel hardliner channels Trump in bid to end Colombia's leftist era in pivotal election
events.description: Colombia's next president could reshape U.S. counternarcotics cooperation as voters choose between security-first and dialogue-based approaches.

events.title: Israeli ambassador compares France's far-left leader's rhetoric to Hitler as antisemitism surges
events.description: France recorded 1,320 antisemitic acts in 2025, up from 436 in 2022, as Jewish families alter daily habits to avoid being identified, CRIF's president says.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Israeli ambassador compares France's far-left leader's rhetoric to Hitler as antisemitism surges
